COURT REPORTING SERVICES LESS THAN 1 HOUR RE: DEC-14-20 DEMURRER (TRANSACTION ID # 100116105) FILED BY DEFENDANT LABOR COMMISSIONER

LAW AND MOTION DEPT. 302: DEFENDANT LABOR COMMISSIONER'S DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 430.41. BEFORE FILING A DEMURRER, THE DEMURRING PARTY MUST "MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE WITH THE PARTY WHO FILED THE PLEADING THAT IS SUBJECT TO DEMURRER FOR THE PURPOSE OF DETERMINING WHETHER AN AGREEMENT CAN BE REACHED THAT WOULD RESOLVE THE OBJECTIONS TO BE RAISED IN THE DEMURRER." (CODE CIV. PROC., SEC. 430.41(A).) DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO SUBMIT A DECLARATION SHOWING THAT IT COMPLIED WITH THIS REQUIREMENT BEFORE FILING THE DEMURRER. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IMMEDIATELY. DEFENDANT'S RESPONSE TO THE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY JANUARY 14, 2021. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: PROCEEDINGS NOT REPORTED.

Off Calendar Feb-19-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Labor Commissioner's demurrers to the third amended complaint (TAC) are sustained with leave to amend. The TAC's allegations are largely directed at a non-party - the San Francisco Superior Court - and not the Labor Commissioner.

Order is signed.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-03-2020 Continued To Feb-03-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to 3RD Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-14-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance is made in this matter.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT LABOR COMMISSIONER's DEMURRER to Third Amended COMPLAINT is ordered off calendar for failure to comply with Code of Civil Procedure section 430.41. Before filing a demurrer, the demurring party must "meet and confer in person or by telephone with the party who filed the pleading that is subject to demurrer for the purpose of determining whether an agreement can be reached that would resolve the objections to be raised in the demurrer." (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 430.41(a).) Defendant has failed to submit a declaration showing that it complied with this requirement before filing the demurrer. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately. Defendant's response to the Third Amended Complaint is now due by January 14, 2021.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: proceedings not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-05-2020 Continued To Dec-23-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to 2ND Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-30-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT LILIA GARCIA-BROWER's DEMURRER to 2ND Amended COMPLAINT:

The Labor Commissioner's demurrer to the second amended complaint is sustained. On September 4, 2020, plaintiff attempted to file a third amended complaint. However, plaintiff did not obtain leave of court, and thus that pleading was not properly filed or served. (See Code Civ. Proc. sec. 472(a) [any pleading may be amended once at any time before the answer or demurrer is filed, or after the demurrer is filed but before it is heard if the amended pleading is filed and served no later than the date for filing an opposition to the demurrer].) Plaintiff is granted 10 days leave to amend, and is admonished that future filings must be timely and with required leave of court.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
